input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to specify values for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device theme at startup and listens for changes — no extra wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing:
var page = this;

page.SetAppThemeColor(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

var heading = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
heading.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#000000"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For non-color properties, use the generic SetAppTheme<T>:
heading.SetAppTheme<string>(Label.TextProperty,
    light: "Light Mode",
    dark:  "Dark Mode");
```

---

**Key points:**

| | XAML | C# |
|---|---|---|
| Syntax | `{AppThemeBinding Light=…, Dark=…}` | `.SetAppThemeColor()` / `.SetAppTheme<T>()` |
| Auto-updates on theme change | ✅ | ✅ |
| Supports any BindableProperty | ✅ | ✅ |

- **`Default`** — both XAML and C# accept an optional `Default` value used when the theme is indeterminate.
- **Resource dictionaries** — for larger apps, define theme colors in `App.xaml` as `AppThemeBinding` resources to avoid repeating hex values:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>

<!-- Usage -->
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{StaticResource PageBg}" />
```

No platform-specific code is required — MAUI handles the `AppInfo.RequestedTheme` subscription automatically.
